Class Java

java.lang.Object
uno.anahata.asi.agi.tool.ToolContext
uno.anahata.asi.agi.tool.AnahataToolkit
uno.anahata.asi.toolkit.java.Java
All Implemented Interfaces:
ContextProvider, PropertyChangeSource, Rebindable
Direct Known Subclasses:
SwingJava
public class Java extends AnahataToolkit
A powerful toolkit for compiling and executing Java code dynamically within the application's JVM. It provides a "hot-reload" capability by using a child-first classloader and supports context-aware execution through the OnTheFlyAgiTool base class.
Author:
anahata

    Represents a node in the hierarchical tree of system properties.

    Method
    Description
    Agi
    getAgi()
    Gets the parent AGI session this context provider is registered under.

    Gets the designated position of this provider's content in the AI prompt.

  • Field Details

    • parentFirstClassess

      protected final Set<String> parentFirstClassess
      A set of infrastructure classes that MUST always be loaded by the parent classloader (the ASI engine) to preserve static state and ThreadLocal context. This prevents "Identity Crisis" issues where a child-loaded script cannot access the engine's context.

    • defaultCompilerClasspath

      public String defaultCompilerClasspath
      The base compiler and classloader classpath. Extra entries can be provided at execution time. This serves as the foundation for both dynamic compilation and the child-first classloader logic.

    • classpathPrinter

      protected transient VeryPrettyClassPathPrinter classpathPrinter
      The transient printer used to generate token-optimized classpath manifests. It is recreated lazily to ensure it is always available after deserialization.

  • Constructor Details

    • Java

      public Java()
      Default constructor. Initializes the default classpath from the system's "java.class.path" property.

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public void initialize()
      Performs initial setup for a newly created toolkit instance.

      This method is called exactly once by the ToolManager after the toolkit has been instantiated and registered. It provides a clean hook for toolkits to perform one-time initialization logic that requires the tool environment to be fully configured, such as scanning for project instructions or registering default resources.

      Empty implementation as per architectural refinement. Initialization logic is moved to postActivate() and setDefaultClasspath(String).

      Overrides:
      initialize in class AnahataToolkit

    • postActivate

      public void postActivate()
      Callback method triggered by the Asi Container once the Agi session has been fully deserialized and associated to the ASI container.

      Synchronizes the classpath printer with the current default classpath after the toolkit has been activated or deserialized.

      Overrides:
      postActivate in class AnahataToolkit

    • getSystemInstructions

      public List<String> getSystemInstructions() throws Exception
      Gets a list of system instruction strings provided by this context provider. These are typically prepended to the conversation as high-level guidance.
      Returns:
      A list of system instruction strings.
      Throws:
      Exception - if an error occurs during instruction generation.

    • registerParentFirstClass

      public final void registerParentFirstClass(Class<?> c)
      Registers a class and all its superclasses and interfaces to be loaded by the parent classloader.
      Parameters:
      c - The class to register.

    • getConcreteClassModelShouldExtend

      protected Class<? extends ToolContext> getConcreteClassModelShouldExtend()
      The class the model should extend when generating Agi tools.

      In this base implementation, it returns OnTheFlyAgiTool, which provides the necessary context anchors (log, error, etc.) for the script.

      Returns:
      the base AgiTool class.

    • getDefaultClasspath

      public String getDefaultClasspath()
      Gets the current default classpath used for compilation and class loading.
      Returns:
      The full default classpath string.

    • setDefaultClasspath

      public void setDefaultClasspath(String defaultCompilerClasspath)
      Sets the default classpath for the compiler and classloader.
      Parameters:
      defaultCompilerClasspath - The new default classpath string.

    • getPrettyPrintedDefaultClasspath

      public String getPrettyPrintedDefaultClasspath()
      Returns a token-efficient, pretty-printed version of the default classpath.

      Implementation note: This leverages lexical grouping and version promotion to keep the classpath manifest small.

      Returns:
      The pretty-printed classpath string.

    • getClasspathPrinter

      protected final VeryPrettyClassPathPrinter getClasspathPrinter()
      Gets the lazily-initialized classpath printer.
      Returns:
      The VeryPrettyClassPathPrinter instance.

    • createClassPathPrinter

      protected VeryPrettyClassPathPrinter createClassPathPrinter()
      Factory method to create the specialized classpath printer.
      Returns:
      A new VeryPrettyClassPathPrinter instance.

    • populateMessage

      public void populateMessage(RagMessage ragMessage) throws Exception
      Populates the given RagMessage with dynamic, just-in-time context parts. These parts are appended to the end of the user's prompt (RAG).

      Adds session/container map keys and the abbreviated classpath manifest to the RAG message to provide the model with awareness of its persistent state and available libraries.

      Parameters:
      ragMessage - The message to be augmented with context.
      Throws:
      Exception - if an error occurs during context generation.

    • appendMethods

      protected void appendMethods(StringBuilder sb, Class<?> clazz)
      Appends the signatures of all declared methods of a class to a StringBuilder, filtering out standard Object methods and internal lambda/abstract cruft.
      Parameters:
      sb - The StringBuilder to append to.
      clazz - The class to inspect.

    • compile

      public Class compile(String sourceCode, String className, String extraClassPath, String[] compilerOptions) throws ClassNotFoundException, NoSuchMethodException, IllegalAccessException, InvocationTargetException
      Compiles Java source code into a Class object using the system's Java compiler.
      Parameters:
      sourceCode - The Java source code to compile.
      className - The fully qualified name of the class.
      extraClassPath - Additional classpath entries to include.
      compilerOptions - Additional options for the Java compiler.
      Returns:
      The compiled Class object.
      Throws:
      ClassNotFoundException - if the class cannot be found after compilation.
      NoSuchMethodException - if a required method is missing.
      IllegalAccessException - if access to a member is denied.
      InvocationTargetException - if a method invocation fails.

    • findClassFallbackBytes

      protected byte[] findClassFallbackBytes(String name)
      A hook for subclasses to provide class bytes if the standard loading flow fails.

      This is used by the NetBeans implementation (NbJava) to bridge Multi-Release JAR classes into the memory-based loader.

      Parameters:
      name - The FQN of the class.
      Returns:
      The class bytes, or null if not found.

    • compileAndExecute

      public Object compileAndExecute(String sourceCode, String extraClassPath, String[] compilerOptions) throws Exception
      Compiles and executes a Java class named 'Anahata' on the application's JVM. The class must extend OnTheFlyAgiTool and implement Callable.
      Parameters:
      sourceCode - The Java source code to compile and execute.
      extraClassPath - Additional classpath entries.
      compilerOptions - Additional compiler options.
      Returns:
      The result of the execution.
      Throws:
      Exception - if compilation or execution fails.

    • getSystemProperties

      public String getSystemProperties() throws Exception
      Generates a token-efficient, hierarchical representation of all JVM system properties (excluding the classpath itself).
      Returns:
      A formatted string of system properties.
      Throws:
      Exception - if an error occurs.

    • renderSysProp

      private void renderSysProp(StringBuilder sb, Java.SystemPropertyNode node, int indent)
      Recursively renders a system property node and its children into a formatted string.
      Parameters:
      sb - The StringBuilder to append to.
      node - The node to render.
      indent - The current indentation level.
